name: AppWorld-UL: Benchmarking Diverse Agent-User Interactions for Tool-Use
summary: arXiv:2607.20536v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-use agents that address day-to-day digital tasks such as ordering groceries must not only operate applications, but also interact with the user,...
description: arXiv:2607.20536v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-use agents that address day-to-day digital tasks such as ordering groceries must not only operate applications, but also interact with the user, e.g., to ask clarification questions, prompt for confirmation, and inform the user when the instruction is infeasible. However, current benchmarks for evaluating agent-user interactions do not captur...
author: Junzhi Chen, Harsh Trivedi, Jane Pan, Michael JQ Zhang, Tejas Srinivasan, Niranjan Balasubramanian, Ashish Sabharwal
